<!-- Generated by GitHub Copilot on 2026-03-15 -->
# Technical Specification: Services Section — Modern Landing Page Redesign

## 1. Overview

Replaces the single hardcoded service card in `LandingPage.razor` with a data-driven 6-card responsive grid. Cards render outcome-focused content loaded from `services.json` and apply hover interactions using the token system from the Hero Section story.

**Key business value:** Clearly enumerated services allow visitors to self-qualify before inquiry, reducing unfit leads.

**Scope and constraints:**
- Content must come from `wwwroot/data/services.json`; no hardcoded copy in the component
- `services.json` currently holds a different schema (`description`, `skills[]`, `detailOrQuote`) that may be consumed by the Agent or MCP server — this spec proposes a backwards-compatible extension
- Icons use CSS-based Unicode/SVG sprites or character references; no external icon library dependency is introduced

---

## 2. Data / Content Layer

### 2.1 Schema Change: `wwwroot/data/services.json`

**Current schema** (must remain for backward compatibility):

```json
{
  "description": "...",
  "skills": ["..."],
  "detailOrQuote": "..."
}
```

> **Risk:** The fields `description`, `skills`, and `detailOrQuote` may be consumed by `InterviewCoach.Agent` or `InterviewCoach.Mcp.InterviewData` via HTTP or embedded asset. Do not remove or rename them.

**Proposed addition** — add a `cards` property:

```json
{
  "description": "...",
  "skills": ["..."],
  "detailOrQuote": "...",
  "cards": [
    {
      "icon": "⚙️",
      "name": "Bespoke Software Solutions",
      "description": "Get software shaped precisely to your business, built to scale as you grow."
    },
    {
      "icon": "🤖",
      "name": "AI Integration",
      "description": "Embed Generative AI into your workflows so your team works smarter, not harder."
    },
    {
      "icon": "☁️",
      "name": "Cloud Solutions",
      "description": "Move to the cloud with confidence — resilient, cost-aware architectures from day one."
    },
    {
      "icon": "🚀",
      "name": "MVP Development",
      "description": "Validate your idea fast with a production-ready MVP built for real user feedback."
    },
    {
      "icon": "🔄",
      "name": "Digital Transformation",
      "description": "Modernise legacy systems and processes so your organisation stays competitive."
    },
    {
      "icon": "🛠️",
      "name": "Website Support & Maintenance",
      "description": "Keep your digital presence healthy with proactive support, updates, and monitoring."
    }
  ]
}
```

### 2.2 Blazor Models

Add to WebUI project (e.g. `Services/LandingModels.cs`):

```csharp
public record ServiceCardItem(string Icon, string Name, string Description);

public record ServicesData(
    string Description,
    string[] Skills,
    string DetailOrQuote,
    ServiceCardItem[] Cards
);
```

### 2.3 Data Loading

In `LandingPage.razor` code-behind:

```csharp
ServicesData? servicesData;

protected override async Task OnInitializedAsync()
{
    siteProps = await Http.GetFromJsonAsync<SiteProperties>("/data/site-properties.json");
    servicesData = await Http.GetFromJsonAsync<ServicesData>("/data/services.json");
}
```

> Both calls can run concurrently using `Task.WhenAll` to reduce load time:
> ```csharp
> (siteProps, servicesData) = await (
>     Http.GetFromJsonAsync<SiteProperties>("/data/site-properties.json"),
>     Http.GetFromJsonAsync<ServicesData>("/data/services.json")
> ).WhenBoth();
> ```
> Or more idiomatically:
> ```csharp
> var t1 = Http.GetFromJsonAsync<SiteProperties>("/data/site-properties.json");
> var t2 = Http.GetFromJsonAsync<ServicesData>("/data/services.json");
> await Task.WhenAll(t1, t2);
> siteProps = t1.Result;
> servicesData = t2.Result;
> ```

---

## 3. UI Component

### 3.1 File Changes

| File | Change |
|------|--------|
| `wwwroot/data/services.json` | Add `cards` property |
| `Components/Pages/LandingPage.razor` | Replace hardcoded card with `@foreach` over `servicesData.Cards`; add `id="services"` anchor |
| `Components/Pages/LandingPage.razor.css` | Replace `.services-*` styles with token-based card grid styles |

### 3.2 Markup Structure

```razor
<section id="services" class="services-section" aria-labelledby="services-heading">
    <div class="section-inner">
        <h2 id="services-heading" class="section-title reveal-item" style="--reveal-delay: 0ms">
            Our Services
        </h2>

        @if (servicesData?.Cards is { Length: > 0 })
        {
            <ul class="services-grid" role="list">
                @foreach (var (card, index) in servicesData.Cards.Select((c, i) => (c, i)))
                {
                    <li class="service-card reveal-item"
                        style="--reveal-delay: @(index * 80)ms">
                        <span class="service-icon" aria-hidden="true">@card.Icon</span>
                        <h3 class="service-name">@card.Name</h3>
                        <p class="service-description">@card.Description</p>
                    </li>
                }
            </ul>
        }
    </div>
</section>
```

> **Note on icons:** Using emoji characters as icons avoids an external icon library dependency at this stage. If the team moves to SVG icons, the `icon` field in `services.json` should store an SVG name/id reference and the component updated to render an `<svg><use>` element with `aria-hidden="true"`.

### 3.3 CSS — Services Section (`LandingPage.razor.css`)

```css
.services-section {
    padding: 5rem 2rem;
    background: var(--color-surface-page);
}

.section-inner {
    max-width: 1200px;
    margin: 0 auto;
}

.section-title {
    font-size: clamp(1.75rem, 4vw, 2.5rem);
    font-weight: 700;
    color: var(--color-text-primary);
    text-align: center;
    margin: 0 0 3rem;
}

.services-grid {
    display: grid;
    grid-template-columns: repeat(3, 1fr);
    gap: 1.5rem;
    list-style: none;
    padding: 0;
    margin: 0;
}

.service-card {
    background: var(--color-surface-card);
    border-radius: var(--radius-md);
    padding: 2rem 1.5rem;
    box-shadow: var(--shadow-card);
    border: 1px solid transparent;
    transition:
        box-shadow var(--motion-normal),
        border-color var(--motion-normal),
        transform var(--motion-normal);
    display: flex;
    flex-direction: column;
    gap: 0.75rem;
}

@media (hover: hover) {
    .service-card:hover {
        transform: translateY(-4px);
        box-shadow: var(--shadow-lift);
        border-color: var(--color-accent-border);
    }

    .service-card:hover .service-icon {
        filter: saturate(2) brightness(0.9);
    }
}

.service-icon {
    font-size: 2rem;
    line-height: 1;
    display: block;
    transition: filter var(--motion-normal);
}

.service-name {
    font-size: 1.1rem;
    font-weight: 600;
    color: var(--color-text-primary);
    margin: 0;
}

.service-description {
    font-size: 0.95rem;
    color: var(--color-text-secondary);
    line-height: 1.6;
    margin: 0;
    flex: 1;
}

/* Responsive */
@media (max-width: 1023px) {
    .services-grid {
        grid-template-columns: repeat(2, 1fr);
    }
}

@media (max-width: 767px) {
    .services-grid {
        grid-template-columns: 1fr;
    }
}
```

---

## 4. Testing Requirements

### Data Loading Tests (Unit — xUnit)

| Test | Setup | Action | Expected |
|------|-------|--------|----------|
| JSON deserialises to model | Read actual `services.json` fixture | `JsonSerializer.Deserialize<ServicesData>` | `Cards.Length == 6`; no null fields |
| Cards array not null when missing from JSON | JSON without `cards` key | Deserialise | `Cards` is empty array, not null (use `[]` default) |

### UI Tests (Playwright)

| Test | Action | Expected |
|------|--------|----------|
| Section visible on initial render | Navigate to `/` | `#services` is visible without requiring scroll interaction |
| Six cards render | Navigate to `/` | Exactly 6 `.service-card` elements present |
| Cards have name, icon, description | Inspect each card | Each has `.service-icon`, `.service-name`, `.service-description` |
| Section has correct `id` | Inspect DOM | `<section id="services">` exists |
| No hardcoded text | Inspect HTML | No copy matching the old single hardcoded card description |
| Hover card on desktop | Hover `.service-card:first-child` | Card border-color is not `transparent` |
| Cards link-free | Inspect DOM | No `<a>` inside `.service-card` (cards are not links) |
| No overflow at 375px | Set viewport 375px | Single-column; no horizontal scrollbar |
| Icons hidden from a11y tree | Inspect DOM | Each `.service-icon` has `aria-hidden="true"` |

---

## 5. Implementation Notes

### Architectural Decisions

- **Backwards-compatible `cards` addition to `services.json`:** Existing consumers of `description`, `skills`, and `detailOrQuote` are unaffected. If confirmed that no consumer reads `services.json` from the WebUI project, the file can be fully restructured in a follow-up.
- **`<ul role="list">` for card grid:** Improves screen reader experience (announces count of items) while using CSS grid for layout. Safari resets list semantics on `list-style: none`; `role="list"` restores them explicitly.
- **`@media (hover: hover)` for hover effects:** Ensures hover styles only apply on devices with precise pointing input. Touch devices never see a stuck hover state.
- **Emoji icons as interim solution:** Zero dependency, fully accessible when wrapped in `aria-hidden`. Should be replaced with a proper SVG system if branding requires it.

### Dependencies & Build Order

1. Update `wwwroot/data/services.json` — add `cards` array
2. Add `ServiceCardItem` and `ServicesData` records to WebUI models
3. Update `LandingPage.razor` — parallel data load, `@foreach` card loop, `id="services"` anchor
4. Update `LandingPage.razor.css` — grid and card styles

### Edge Cases & Error Handling

- If `services.json` fails to load: section renders empty (no cards); no error thrown. Consider adding a loading state or fallback UI if the product requires it.
- If `Cards` is null in JSON: model should default to an empty array via `[JsonPropertyName("cards")] public ServiceCardItem[] Cards { get; init; } = [];`
- If fewer than 6 cards exist in the file: grid renders however many exist; the AC of "exactly 6" is a content QA concern, not a code concern

### Performance Considerations

- Fetch `services.json` in parallel with `site-properties.json` using `Task.WhenAll`
- Grid uses CSS `grid` with equal `1fr` columns rather than `flex`; browser calculates column widths in a single pass

---

## Prerequisites

- [Hero Section Spec](./hero-section-spec.md) — CSS design tokens (`--color-accent-border`, `--shadow-card`, `--shadow-lift`, `--motion-normal`, `--radius-md`, `--color-surface-*`, `--color-text-*`) and `.reveal-item` system must be in place
